mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing starts above 1.2 million units signals a strengthening demand for residential construction, which boosts consumer confidence and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with HOUST > 1.2M are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production below 108 signals a weakening supply chain, which reduces inventory turnover and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO < 108 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in initial unemployment claims above 250K signals a weakening labor market, which reduces consumer spending and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with PAYEMS < 150K and UNRATE > 4.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real disposable personal income above 4% signals a strengthening demand for consumer goods, which boosts retail sales and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with RSAFS > 4% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the consumer price index below 2.5 signals a weakening inflationary pressure, which reduces the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with CPIAUCSL < 2.5 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the GDP below 2% signals a weakening supply chain, which reduces inventory turnover and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with GDPC1 < 2% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals a weakening labor market, which reduces consumer spending and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with UNRATE > 4.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
